narrative.narrative: Extreme fear is gripping the market as the Fear & Greed Index plunges to 25, yet stablecoin reserves are quietly expanding—a classic divergence that historically precedes accumulation. This setup mirrors the late summer 2024 bottom, where sidelined capital eventually rotated back in once the panic exhausted itself. The current risk-off regime is testing conviction, but the growing stablecoin powder keg suggests the next move may catch the fearful off guard.
narrative.headline: Stablecoins Swell While Fear Grips the Market

consensus.key_reasoning: The live Fear & Greed score of 25 places the market squarely in the Panic rung (24-31), but the analyst's call of Anxiety (39-46) is illegal as it sits two rungs away; therefore, the phase is anchored down to Panic, with the leg rising toward Anxiety as stablecoin growth signals early accumulation. The pattern match to the 2024 August bottom is strong, but with 8 diverging factors and a risk-off regime, aggressive longs are premature, warranting a cautious stance.
disagreements_noted: Analyst calls Anxiety (score 39-46) but live Fear & Greed is 25 (Panic band 24-31) — phase adjusted to Panic per ±1 rung rule
Narrative frames this as an accumulation bottom (bullish) while analyst flags RISK_OFF with 8 diverging factors (bearish/cautious)
Narrative confidence (0.79) notably higher than analyst confidence (0.62) despite broader factor divergence
